text: **Anna:** Hi there {sports|スポーツ|/spɔːrts/|I love watching sports.} fans!
**Anna:** {Baseball|野球|/ˈbeɪsbɔːl/|Baseball is popular in America.} is America's sport.
**Anna:** Today I am going to {Nationals|ナショナルズ/国民の|/ˈnæʃənəlz/|Nationals Park is in DC.} Park!
**Anna:** It is home to Washington DC's baseball team, the Washington Nationals.
**Anna:** I am taking a {bus|バス|/bʌs/|The bus is coming soon.} to a nationals baseball game.
**Ashley:** A {taxi|タクシー|/ˈtæksi/|I called a taxi.} is faster than a bus.
**Anna:** You know, I {love|愛する/大好き|/lʌv/|I love baseball.} baseball.
**Anna:** In fact, I wanted to be a baseball {player|選手|/ˈpleɪər/|He is a famous player.}
**Jonathan:** Is this one of your memories?
**Anna:** I am at {home plate|本塁|/hoʊm pleɪt/|He touched home plate.}
**Anna:** For the {pitch|投球|/pɪtʃ/|The pitch was fast.}
**Anna:** I run to first base, second base, third base, then home plate.
**Anna:** It's a {home run|本塁打|/hoʊm rʌn/|He hit a home run.}!
**Anna:** The crowd {jeers|野次る/ヤジる|/dʒɪərz/|The crowd jeers the losing team.}!
**Jonathan:** Have fun at the {game|試合/ゲーム} Anna.
**Anna:** I am going to see a {nationals|国民の/ナショナルズ} baseball game!
**Ashley:** Do you have a {ticket|チケット} ?
**Ashley:** Anna, you should go {early|早く/前もって}.
**Anna:** That is why I'm taking a {taxi|タクシー}.
**Ashley:** Taxi Anna, Anna, that is not a good idea
**Ashley:** A {bicycle|自転車} is faster than a taxi.
**Anna:** In DC {traffic|交通/渋滞}, sometimes a bicycle is faster than a car.
**Anna:** And right now there is a lot of traffic.
**Anna:** But I don't have a bicycle.
**Ashley:** Okay, Anna, you can use my bicycle.
**Anna:** I really want to {learn|学ぶ/習得する} how to ride one!
**Anna:** Well, what do you mean {learn|学ぶ/習得する} how to ride a bike?
**Ashley:** You know, Anna, the metro is {faster|速い/早い|/ˈfeɪstər/|This car is faster than that one.} than a taxi and a bicycle.
**Ashley:** You {ought to|〜すべきだ/〜するはずだ|/ˈɔːt tə/|You ought to study harder.} take the metro.
**Anna:** The metro was a lot {faster|速い/早い|/ˈfeɪstər/|This car is faster than that one.} than a bus, taxi, or bike.
**Anna:** And now I'm at {National Park|国立公園|/ˈnæʃənəl pɑːrk/|We visited National Park yesterday.}
**Anna:** Look at all these {fans|ファン/応援者|/fænz/|Many fans waited for the singer.}
**Anna:** So many people like to watch baseball.
**Anna:** There's the {ticket window|切符売り場|/ˈtɪkɪt ˈwɪndoʊ/|I bought tickets at the ticket window.}
**Anna:** The tickets are {sold out|売り切れ|/soʊld aʊt/|The concert was sold out quickly.}
**Anna:** But I really want to watch a baseball game
**Anna:** Does anybody have an {extra|余分の/追加の|/ˈekstrə/|I have an extra pencil.} ticket?
**Fan:** Next time, you should buy your ticket {online|オンラインで|/ˈɒnlaɪn/|I bought the tickets online.}
**Anna:** Being early is better than being late.
